page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It features 12 dedicated physical cores with a single thread per core, providing 12 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 96.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU accelerators, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps.

Benchmark results indicate a clear distinction between single-core and multi-core performance. Single-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the bottom 25 percent, and single-threaded gzip compression is in the bottom 10 percent. However, multi-core performance scales to average levels, as shown by stress-ng multi-core and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes also register in the average tier, though the Passmark memory score is relatively weak.

The primary tradeoff of the ecs.r5.3xlarge is its modest single-threaded compute capability compared to its generous memory allocation. From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, this instance is designed for memory-intensive workloads that do not require high-frequency single-core processing. It is well-suited for database operations, caching layers like Redis, multi-threaded data processing, and general-purpose enterprise applications requiring a larger memory footprint without the overhead of local storage.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 12 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor at 2.5 GHz and 96.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under a KVM hypervisor, it provides a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 4 Gbps. Benchmarks reveal weak single-core CPU and memory performance, but average multi-core processing, memory bandwidth, and Redis SET throughput. This profile makes the ecs.r5.3xlarge qualitatively cost-effective for memory-intensive workloads that benefit from a large RAM footprint but do not demand high single-threaded compute performance. It is best suited for database hosting, caching layers, and multi-threaded enterprise applications.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.r5.3xlarge is a memory-optimized instance with 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 96.0 GB of RAM, and 4 Gbps network bandwidth, designed for memory-intensive workloads without local storage.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio and dedicated Intel Xeon processing for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 12 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs with 96.0 GB of RAM
High memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core
4 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with KVM virtualization
Average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Suited for databases, caching layers, and multi-threaded workloads
